rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/26 pursuant to the attorney's "status report." (It is unclear why the conservator does not have bank records for this accounting period, which was while the conservatee was still living. A final accounting in a conservatorship must include an accounting for the period subsequent to the date of death, as required by Probate Code Section 2620(b) and as ordered on 11/7/25.) No estate appears to have been opened yet for the deceased conservatee.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES RAY THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/11/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The supplement filed 01/13/2026 requests relief that differs from the underlying petition, and is considered an amendment. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53(a)). 2) Please provide authority for any distribution to Theodore Thomas, who predeceased decedent and was not survived by issue. Decedent died intestate. Please clarify the reasoning for a distribution to Theodore Thomas. Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 02/25/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CESAR CACCAM CERVANTES JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No petition has been filed yet, as ordered on 2/26/24 and as directed by the judge at the hearing on 9/29/25 to be done by 11/3/25. 2) The decedent died on 7/25/23. If the heirs do not waive an accounting, the first two-year accounting is already overdue. [LRSF 14.34 A.] The Court might consider suspending or removing the administrator and appointing the Public Administrator to complete estate administration. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Refiled, Revised And Correct Petition To Contest Living Trust Of Gerald Deryan Probate Code 17200
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition is premature for the court's review, as the petition failed to meet the fundamental requirements as follows: (1) At least 30 days before the hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause Notice of Hearing and a copy of the petition to be given to the persons who are entitled to. Not all trust beneficiaries are listed on the petition and properly noticed. Also, attaching a copy of USPS mailing records is insufficient. (2) Notice of Direction for Remote Appearances and Access (LRSF 14.1.C.6) to be given to the persons who are entitled to. (3) Clarification of whether the current trustee lives in another state, and how this state is the proper venue. (4) No proposed order submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUCITA GARCIA FLORES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/26. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. A competing petition for probate of a more recently executed will was filed, but Petitioner has not filed a will contest. 2. No copy of the will or a full written statement of the testamentary words is attached to the petition. A copy of the will or full statement must also be filed separately in the same way as an original will is filed. 3. Petitioner has not alleged what priority she has to serve as administrator. 4. Eduardsita Flores and Feliciano A. Garcia are alleged to be named as the successor co-executors but no declinations to serve by either of them have been filed. 5. Eduardsita Flores and Eduardo S. Flores Jr were not given notice. 6. Bond is requested to be waived without any basis. 7. Relationships to decedent are missing in Item 8 of the petition. 8. The trustee of the trust that is allegedly the beneficiary of this estate is not listed / identified in Item 8. If the trustee and proposed administrator are the same, the trust beneficiaries must also be listed / identified. Notice to all trustees and trust beneficiaries are also required, if not provided already. 9. Duties and Liabilities have not been filed. 10. Confidential Supplement to Duties and Liabilities have not been filed. 11. Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Calendar Matter: EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR GOOD CAUSE EXCEPTION TO NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF TEMPORARY CONSERVATOR OF THE PERSON ESTATE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing is continued to Friday, January 30, 2026 at 10:00 a.m.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 08/09/2027.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TSUTOMU MATANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/11/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States (Probate Code Section 8113). 2) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 02/02/2026 to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BOCK HOY LOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 08/09/2027. MICHAEL KASOLAS will be appointed probate referee. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MERRILEE ANN MORROW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 7/26/27. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 7/26/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.
